page: Amazon Web Services m6id.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the m6id.4xlarge in the average tier for its hardware class. In stress-ng tests, it achieves a single-core CPU score of 1892.09 and a multi-core score of 15478.65. Memory bandwidth tests show 834.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 97.12 GB/sec for large-block reads and 56.75 GB/sec for large-block writes. Geekbench results include a single-core score of 1642.0 and a multi-core score of 9753.0, with software compilation scoring 15354.0 and ray tracing at 17687.0. For database workloads, Passmark database operations score 7799.66, and Redis SET operations reach 15.95 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at 22.43 GB/sec.

The inclusion of 950 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage alongside a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or caching without the added cost of external block storage. However, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy machine learning training, though it can handle light LLM inference, processing 1122.4 tokens per second on a small 135M model and 32.05 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results show solid mid-range performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9753.0, a Passmark CPU score of 21082.62, and Redis SET performance reaching 15.95 million operations per second. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring high-speed local storage alongside balanced compute resources. It is highly suitable for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.

64.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
